rulings.Case Title: SYED SHAMSUDDOHA ET AL VS. AUTOZONERS, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Renewed Motion To Lift Stay Pursuant To Cal. Code. Civ. Proc. Section 1008(B); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, May 7, 2021, Line 12, PLAINTIFFS SYED SHAMSUDDOHA and MIRIAM HERNANDEZ's Renewed Motion To Lift Stay Pursuant To Cal. Code. Civ. Proc. Section 1008(B): Plaintiffs' motion to lift stay per CCP 1008(b) is denied without prejudice to re-filing after the scope of Alvarez v. AutoZone Inc. is clarified. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: SYNERGY PROJECT MANAGEMENT, INC. VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Compliance And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Friday, May 7, 2021, Line 1, PLAINTIFF SYNERGY PROJECT MANAGEMENT, INC.'s Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Compliance And Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge William Lynn, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. Parties should be prepared to discuss sufficiency of meet and confer efforts by both parties and the impact of discovery cut-off. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to williamclynn@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JACQUELINE HOLDEN VS. MAVENTUS GROUP INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For New Trial
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, May 7, 2021, Line 13, PLAINTIFF JACQUELINE HOLDEN's Motion For New Trial: Plaintiff Jacqueline Holden moves for a new trial after losing a summary judgment motion. The new trial motion, which makes three arguments, is denied. First, Holden says "the court's denial of a continuance was an irregularity and abuse of discretion." In fact, I granted a continuance. After parting ways with her lawyer in mid-November 2020, Holden blew the deadline for opposing the summary judgment motion. I nonetheless continued the summary judgment hearing a month and allowed Holden a late opposition, which she filed. Second, Holden claims "newly discovered evidence" - a purchase agreement. But Holden concedes she and her counsel possessed this document all along. Third, Holden argues that summary judgment was improper because her opposition said "there are well over 20 disputed facts." A litigant's labeling a fact "disputed" does not make it so - or make it material. I considered both sides' evidence and found no triable issue of material fact. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER M BORER VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint With Court Reporter Fee
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, May 7, 2021, Line 4, 2. PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER BORER's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint: Plaintiff Christopher Borer's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is granted. California courts "are bound to apply a policy of great liberality in permitting amendments to the complaint at any stage of the proceeding, up to and including trial." (Atkinson v. Elk Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 739, 761.) The current trial date is five months out and the added discovery defendants say they "may" take appears minimal. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: BARRY FORMAN VS. TIMOTHY COVINGTON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, May 7, 2021, Line 9, 1. DEFENDANT JOHN MATTHESEN's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT: Defendant John Matthesen's demurrer is ordered off calendar for failure to comply with CCP 430.41, which requires a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before filing a demurrer. CCP 430.41(a) expressly requires a second such meet and confer after an amended pleading is filed. The parties are ordered to immediately meet and confer and defendant Matthesen's response to the amended complaint is now due by June 2, 2021. =(302/RBU)
